requirements.description: AI-quota rate limiter is atomic under real concurrency
requirements.evidence: CRITICAL FINDING, FIXED AND VERIFIED. checkRateLimit() in src/lib/ai/rate-limit.ts did a non-atomic SELECT-COUNT-then-insert-later check. Real adversarial attack: 10 parallel requests against /api/ai/analyze-resume (free-tier limit 3/24h) on live production -> 10/10 succeeded (real OpenAI calls). Fixed by routing through the existing atomic rate_limit_increment() Postgres RPC (same one already used correctly by the non-AI Postgres rate limiter). Re-ran identical attack against the fixed, redeployed production build: exactly 3/10 succeeded, 7/10 correctly rejected with 429-equivalent. This affects every route using checkRateLimit: analyze-resume, audit-portfolio, generate-portfolio, improve-resume, role-match, jobs/import, jobs/match, jobs/[id]/tailor, ats/check.

requirements.description: Build-time server-only enforcement on every secret-holding module
requirements.evidence: None of src/lib/supabase/server.ts, src/lib/ai/client.ts, src/lib/ai/openai.ts, src/lib/ai/gemini.ts, src/lib/jobs/providers/index.ts, src/lib/stripe/client.ts had an explicit import 'server-only' guard. None were actually reachable from client code today (verified: build succeeds, which it would not if a client bundle imported a server-only-guarded module) but there was no enforced guarantee against a future accidental import. Added the guard to all six; typecheck/lint/build all still pass.

requirements.description: Global AI-spend ceiling independent of per-user limits
requirements.evidence: Gap: per-user limits don't bound aggregate spend if an attacker spreads calls across many free accounts, each individually within its own quota. Added a platform-wide daily atomic counter (AI_GLOBAL_DAILY_LIMIT, default 2000/day) checked before the per-user counter in checkRateLimit(), using the same rate_limit_increment() RPC.

requirements.description: Arbitrary client-supplied Price ID cannot redirect checkout to an unintended price
requirements.evidence: Live attack: POSTed {plan:'monthly', priceId:'price_EVIL_ARBITRARY_INJECTED', price_id:'price_EVIL_ARBITRARY_INJECTED'} to /api/stripe/create-checkout-session as a real authenticated user. Got 200 with a real Stripe checkout URL — the route ignores any client-supplied price field entirely and maps plan to a server-configured env-var price ID.

requirements.description: Independent re-verification of zero server-side user-URL fetches
requirements.evidence: Re-verified independently (not just trusting the prior P1-08 entry): every fetch() call site in src/lib (jobdataapi.ts, external.ts, error-reporter.ts, distributed.ts) uses a fixed, server-configured base URL; user input is only ever appended as a path segment or query param, never used as the origin/host. Zero fetch() calls exist directly in any API route.

requirements.description: Cost controls, incident response, credential rotation docs exist
requirements.evidence: security/COST_CONTROLS.md, security/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md, security/CREDENTIAL_ROTATION.md written this session, cross-referencing real verified state (kill switches, atomic rate limiter, BLOCKED items honestly marked where dashboard access wasn't available).

requirements.description: npm audit reviewed, no critical/high vulnerabilities
requirements.evidence: 3 moderate-severity findings, all the same PostCSS XSS advisory (GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93) bundled inside Next.js's own internal node_modules/next/node_modules/postcss (v8.4.31) — confirmed every current Next release including 16.3.0 canary builds bundles the same version, so no non-breaking upstream fix exists yet. Not user-reachable: postcss here is a build-time CSS tool, never processes user-controlled CSS strings at runtime. The suggested 'npm audit fix --force' would downgrade Next 16.2.9 -> 9.3.3, a catastrophic breaking change explicitly avoided per this audit's instruction not to blindly force dependency upgrades. Accepted/monitored, zero critical or high severity findings.

requirements.description: API ownership matrix complete, no missing ownership checks
requirements.evidence: security/API_AUTHORIZATION_MATRIX.md — all 21 routes reviewed, zero missing ownership checks

requirements.description: Adversarial cross-user API test suite passes
requirements.evidence: test:authorization 15/15 (found and fixed 2 real PGRST116-caused 500s in jobs/save and applications PATCH)

requirements.description: Stripe portal session ownership enforced
requirements.evidence: Verified by code read + test:authorization: customer_id derived server-side from session, never client input

requirements.description: Cannot publish/export another user's resume or portfolio
requirements.evidence: test:authorization 15/15 covers publish, save, export, tailor, audit, generate-portfolio

requirements.description: Credentials exposed in chat/terminal this session rotated
requirements.evidence: Account owner explicitly confirmed (2026-06-22 release-closure session) that all previously-exposed credentials were rotated in their respective dashboards and updated in .env.local + Vercel: Supabase service-role key, Supabase database password, Stripe secret key, Stripe webhook secret, OpenAI API key, Gemini API key, jobs-provider API key. This confirmation is the account owner's direct statement, not inferred from code or environment-variable presence — no key value was inspected or printed by the assistant at any point.

requirements.description: gitleaks scan clean
requirements.evidence: Installed gitleaks via brew. Found 1 hit (hardcoded sk_test_placeholder fallback string, never a real key) and fixed it to no longer match secret patterns at all. .gitleaks.toml allowlists the now-historical commit. npm run test:secrets: no leaks found.

requirements.description: Truth Ledger fails closed under expanded adversarial set
requirements.evidence: test:truth-ledger 15/15 (existing scope)

requirements.description: Account deletion cascades correctly, no orphaned private data
requirements.evidence: test:deletion 13/13. Was a non-functional stub (toast 'contact support') before this session; built /api/account/delete + real type-DELETE-to-confirm UI. All tables have ON DELETE CASCADE to auth.users; storage cleaned explicitly.

requirements.description: Two-user adversarial RLS isolation
requirements.evidence: test:rls 13/13

requirements.description: Magic-byte verification, size/MIME bucket enforcement
requirements.evidence: test:uploads 8/8

requirements.description: javascript:/data: URI rejection on public portfolio
requirements.evidence: test:xss 4/4

requirements.description: Prompt injection resisted on live AI route
requirements.evidence: test:prompt-injection 9/9

requirements.description: Real checkout -> webhook -> DB entitlement activation works end to end
requirements.evidence: Verified live: real Stripe Checkout, real webhook signature, subscriptions.status=active confirmed via direct DB query

requirements.description: Webhook event idempotency
requirements.evidence: test:stripe-webhook 6/6 — duplicate event ids short-circuited via processed_webhook_events unique constraint, out-of-order events rejected via last_webhook_event_at comparison (migration 010)

requirements.description: Invalid signature / wrong price / spoof rejection tests
requirements.evidence: test:stripe-webhook covers invalid signature; test:stripe-config covers price ID validity; test:authorization covers frontend Pro spoof

requirements.description: Production webhook endpoint registered
requirements.evidence: User authorized a new, separate Vercel project (showcase-app, https://showcase-app-three.vercel.app — distinct from the waitlist-only casefile-ten.vercel.app, never touched). Registered a real Stripe webhook endpoint (we_1TkEgnRrWPEIfq2Mh8VT0W2r) against the deployed /api/stripe/webhook URL. Verified end-to-end with a real, completed monthly checkout (sub_1TkI8vRrWPEIfq2MMzBO89CO, customer cus_UjlfsiaMBM7PdI): Stripe delivered the webhook, signature verified, and the subscriptions row updated correctly (status=active, correct price_id, current_period_end, last_webhook_event_at populated) — confirmed by direct DB query, not just trusting the UI. HONEST CAVEAT: only the monthly path was exercised by a real completed checkout; the annual ($150) checkout session was generated and handed off but not completed by a human, so annual has config-level verification (test:stripe-config confirms the price exists/active) but not a completed live-webhook proof. The webhook handler is price-agnostic (does not branch on monthly vs annual), so risk is low, but this is not the same standard of evidence as monthly.

requirements.description: Distributed rate limiter abstraction with local-dev fallback
requirements.evidence: AI-quota limiter was already Postgres-backed (distributed-safe), just non-atomic — low severity since quotas are generous and same-user races are rare/low-impact. Real gap was the waitlist IP limiter (genuine in-memory Map, single-instance only). Built src/lib/rate-limit/ (Upstash > Postgres-atomic > memory fallback) backed by rate_limit_increment() Postgres function (migration 011, single-statement atomic read+increment). Wired into waitlist/join. test:abuse 3/3 including a real concurrency test: 10 simultaneous requests, exactly 5 allowed — proves atomicity, not just sequential correctness.

requirements.description: Upstash Redis wired for production
Session/cookie/redirect hardening verified via real browser tests
requirements.evidence: src/proxy.ts (Next.js 16 renamed Middleware->Proxy) already correctly refreshed Supabase SSR session cookies and gated protected routes — no rebuild needed there. Found and fixed a real open-redirect: src/app/(auth)/callback/route.ts read `next` from the query string and concatenated it unvalidated into a redirect target. Added safeNextPath() allowlisting same-app relative paths only. test:csrf 6/6 covers logout session invalidation + the open-redirect fix with 4 payload variants.

requirements.description: Origin/Host validation on cookie-authenticated state-changing routes
requirements.evidence: src/lib/security/origin-check.ts + wired into src/proxy.ts for all /api/* POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE (Stripe webhook exempted — its own signature check is the real auth, and it's legitimately cross-origin). test:csrf 6/6: same-origin succeeds, forged cross-origin Origin is rejected 403, GET is never blocked, webhook exemption verified (fails on signature not Origin).

requirements.description: Zero server-side user-URL fetches, regression-tested
requirements.evidence: Code search confirmed zero fetch() calls on user input in any API route

requirements.description: Provider-neutral observability layer
requirements.evidence: src/instrumentation.ts using Next.js's native onRequestError hook (App Router, Next 16) — captures every uncaught server error across Server Components/Route Handlers/Server Actions in one place rather than per-route try/catch. src/lib/observability/error-reporter.ts redacts secret-shaped strings (Stripe keys, webhook secrets, Bearer tokens, JWTs) before logging. Verified redaction directly: a fake error containing a real-shaped sk_live_ key and a JWT was logged with both replaced by [REDACTED].

requirements.description: Error-monitoring adapter wired, app works without it
requirements.evidence: Zero-config path: structured JSON to console.error, captured by any host's log viewer. Optional ERROR_WEBHOOK_URL env var forwards the same redacted payload via a generic POST (works with a Slack webhook now, or a real Sentry/equivalent ingestion endpoint later) — no SDK dependency added, so the app's build/runtime is unaffected whether or not it's configured.

requirements.description: Emergency kill switches for AI/checkout/jobs/publishing
requirements.evidence: src/lib/feature-flags.ts, env-var gated (KILL_SWITCH_AI/CHECKOUT/JOBS_PROVIDER/PUBLISHING), default unset = enabled. Wired at the choke points: checkRateLimit() (covers all 9 AI-quota routes), create-checkout-session, jobs provider factory (forces fixture fallback), portfolio/publish (unpublish never blocked). test:kill-switches 4/4 — spawns real dev server instances with the env var set/unset and proves live behavior actually changes, not just that the code compiles.

requirements.description: Health check endpoints
requirements.evidence: src/app/api/health/route.ts — checks real DB connectivity + config presence (no secret values exposed), exempted from the waitlist lockdown so uptime monitors work regardless of launch state. test:kill-switches covers it (200 ok, no sk_ substring in body).

requirements.description: Backup/restore documented, PITR confirmed
requirements.evidence: security/BACKUP_RESTORE.md. IMPORTANT FINDING: checked the live Supabase org directly (get_organization) — plan is 'free', which has ZERO automated backups and ZERO PITR. This is a real, currently-unmitigated production risk, now documented with the exact upgrade path and an interim manual pg_dump mitigation. 'Documented' is PASS; the underlying backup coverage itself is a separate, real gap surfaced in LAUNCH_CHECKLIST.md as a must-do-before-launch billing decision.

requirements.description: Staging config + deployment docs
requirements.evidence: Updated existing DEPLOYMENT.md (fixed stale AI_API_KEY->OPENAI_API_KEY, added missing LAUNCH_OPEN/annual-price/Upstash env vars, added pre-deploy verification step) and LAUNCH_CHECKLIST.md (reconciled against what's actually done this session, added the backup-tier finding and the deployment-restriction finding). Also discovered a live Vercel deployment exists (casefile-ten.vercel.app) but is reserved for the waitlist only per explicit user instruction — documented as a hard constraint, not used for anything in this work.

requirements.description: Actual staging deployment exists
requirements.evidence: Created new Vercel project 'showcase-app' (distinct from waitlist-only casefile-ten.vercel.app), user-confirmed via AskUserQuestion before any secrets were pushed. Pushed all 13 required env vars (values never printed, verified present via 'vercel env ls' showing names/Encrypted status only). Found and fixed a real deployment bug: the project's auto-detected Framework Preset was 'Other' (silently breaks all dynamic routing/proxy — every route 404'd including static-looking ones), not caused by anything in this app's code; fixed by adding vercel.json with explicit framework:'nextjs'. Disabled Vercel's default SSO deployment-protection wall (user-confirmed) since Stripe webhook delivery and real human checkout both require unauthenticated public access. Live and publicly reachable at https://showcase-app-three.vercel.app — verified /, /waitlist, /login, /pricing, /privacy all return 200, /api/health returns real DB+Stripe+OpenAI config-present checks.

requirements.description: Production SMTP/email readiness
requirements.evidence: security/EMAIL_READINESS.md. Found and fixed a real bug while writing it: signup/login magic-link emailRedirectTo pointed at /auth/callback, which doesn't exist (the (auth) route group is stripped from the URL — the real path is /callback, matching Supabase's documented redirect allowlist). Email confirmation and magic-link sign-in were broken end to end. Also documented: no custom SMTP configured, relying on Supabase's built-in dev-only email service, which is not production-appropriate.

requirements.description: CI security workflow file exists
requirements.evidence: .github/workflows/security.yml, YAML-validated. Static job (typecheck/lint/build/verify.mjs/gitleaks) runs on every push/PR with no secrets needed. Live adversarial-test job (test:rls/authorization/deletion/csrf/abuse/stripe/headers + release:gate) is guarded behind secret presence so it skips cleanly (not fails) until repo secrets are configured — can't actually execute yet, no git remote exists.

requirements.description: Security headers tightened + tested
requirements.evidence: test:headers 13/13. Removed script-src 'unsafe-eval' (verified via real production build + Playwright: signup flow, hydration, zero CSP console violations). Added frame-ancestors 'self' (modern clickjacking protection) and Strict-Transport-Security. Removed a stale/incorrect connect-src entry for api.anthropic.com (app uses OpenAI server-side only; browser never connects to either provider directly).

requirements.description: Account deletion route exists and works
requirements.evidence: Same as P0-08 — built this session, test:deletion 13/13

requirements.description: Privacy/retention documentation
requirements.evidence: security/PRIVACY_RETENTION.md — accurate against the live 19-table schema, documents what's deleted on account deletion vs. preserved (waitlist_signups), AI provider data sharing. Found 107 leftover @example.com test accounts in the live profiles table from this session's testing; cleaned up via scripts/cleanup-test-accounts.mjs (real auth.admin.deleteUser(), user-approved before running). profiles table is back to genuine rows only.

requirements.description: Load test scripts against local dev
requirements.evidence: scripts/load-test.mjs, no new dependency (plain concurrent fetch + percentiles). Run against a real production build at concurrency=50/300 req per endpoint: static pages (waitlist/pricing) ~30-65ms p50-p99, zero errors out of 900 total requests. /api/health is notably slower (p50 529ms, p95 775ms) since it does a real DB round-trip every call — fine at typical uptime-monitor polling intervals, but noted as a real finding, not glossed over.

requirements.description: Full 25-step paid-path E2E run
requirements.evidence: scripts/test-e2e-paid-path.mjs, 6/6 for what it covers: real signup, real billing page, real redirect to a genuine Stripe Checkout session with correct product/price, real test-card field entry, billing-portal route health. HONEST CAVEAT: does NOT complete actual payment submission — investigated directly (field values correct, button enabled, click registers) and concluded Stripe's own bot/fraud detection silently blocks a headless-automated submission, which is appropriate fraud protection, not something to try to defeat. The part this can't reach (webhook -> DB entitlement) is independently proven with real signed events in test-stripe-webhook.mjs, and was manually verified earlier this session (see P0-STRIPE-ENTITLEMENT). A human completing one real checkout manually would close this gap fully.

requirements.description: Axe/Lighthouse pass
requirements.evidence: test:accessibility: 6/6 pages (waitlist/login/signup/pricing/privacy/terms) at ZERO violations of any severity (critical/serious/moderate/minor). Fully resolved this round: normalized all remaining text-muted-foreground opacity modifiers on /waitlist to full opacity (29 color-contrast nodes -> 0), added real <main> landmarks to all 6 public pages (fixed the landmark-one-main and region violations present on every page). No more honest caveats outstanding on this item.

requirements.description: Desktop+mobile screenshot review
requirements.evidence: scripts/capture-screenshots.mjs (gitignored output, regenerate on demand). Reviewed 12 real screenshots (6 pages x desktop/mobile). Found and fixed two real issues: (1) the dev-mode CSP had no carve-out for React's dev-only eval() usage, causing a visible Next.js dev-tools error badge on every page — fixed by making script-src conditionally include unsafe-eval only when NODE_ENV=development, re-verified production stays strict (test:headers still 13/13). (2) Three user-facing surfaces (privacy policy, homepage, in-app resume page) explicitly told users their resume is processed by 'Anthropic's Claude API' — factually wrong, the app uses OpenAI. This is a real privacy-policy accuracy issue, not cosmetic. Fixed all three plus stale references in README.md/SECURITY.md/AI_SETUP.md (the latter was the apparent source, fully rewritten to match the real OpenAI-based architecture).

requirements.description: Interview Lab fails closed behind its own master switch, independent of this gate
requirements.evidence: isInterviewLabRuntimeEnabled() = isGeminiPaidProjectConfirmed() && isGeminiInterviewEnabled() — both must be explicitly true, default unset = disabled. Sub-features (isInterviewLiveEnabled/isInterviewAnalysisEnabled/isInterviewRecordingEnabled) require the master gate AND their own flag. test:interview-live-config covers the fail-closed matrix. Practical effect: every item below describes code that is built and tested, but Interview Lab cannot actually run for real users in production until a human deliberately flips these flags.

requirements.description: Full question-bank coverage across all 10 advertised session types
requirements.evidence: Real bug found and fixed: a stale IMPLEMENTED_SESSION_TYPES allowlist in sessions/route.ts 400'd 7 of 10 session types even after question-bank templates existed for them. Removed the allowlist; buildInterviewPlan() itself fails closed for any genuinely unsupported type. test:interview-plan (loops all 10 types x 3 difficulties) + test:interview-session-types (real create->start->answer->list per type, 40/40).

requirements.description: Story Bank: real UI on top of existing schema/API
requirements.evidence: test:interview-story-bank 11/11, real browser test.

requirements.description: Drills: deterministic (non-AI) scoring, real UI
requirements.evidence: 15 drills, pure textual checks (word count, regex presence/absence) — explicitly not an AI judgment, UI does not claim otherwise. test:interview-drills 40/40 (deterministic) + test:interview-drills-ui 7/7 (real browser).

requirements.description: Private report sharing: zero-anon-RLS-policy + hashed-token pattern, no transcript leak
requirements.evidence: interview_shared_reports has ZERO RLS policy for anon — the only resolution path is resolveSharedReport() via the service role, never a permissive policy. Token is randomBytes(32) base64url (256 bits); only its sha256 hash is persisted; the raw token is shown to the creator exactly once. A leaked DB row alone cannot be used to forge a valid share. Fixed an operator-precedence bug during development (access_count: x ?? 0 + 1 parsed as x ?? (0+1), so the counter never actually incremented) before it shipped. test:interview-sharing 18/18 adversarial.

requirements.description: Recorded Mode upload pipeline: built and tested, deliberately kept disabled
requirements.evidence: 'Architect but keep disabled' per the mission's own instruction — transcription needs the also-disabled Gemini analysis gate, so the route does not fabricate a transcript. Magic-byte audio validation (webm/wav/ogg/mp3), storage-layer file_size_limit/allowed_mime_types on the interview-recordings bucket (not just app-layer), owner-only path-prefix RLS. test:interview-audio-validation 18/18 (deterministic) + test:interview-recording 5/5 (real browser, including the disabled-gate 403).

requirements.description: Account deletion fully cascades Interview Lab data, including nested-path storage
requirements.evidence: Real gap found: the interview-recordings bucket nests one level deeper ($userId/$sessionId/$file) than the existing flat resumes-bucket cleanup pattern could traverse (Supabase Storage's list() is not recursive) — a deleted user's recording files would have been silently orphaned forever. Fixed with a nested-listing loop in /api/account/delete. scripts/test-interview-deletion.mjs (new) seeds a real session/story/drill/share plus a recording file via service role, deletes the account through the real route, and proves every interview_* table is empty AND the nested storage file is genuinely gone: 10/10.

requirements.description: Answer Retry: real attempts preserved, comparison is deterministic only
requirements.evidence: Mission's hard rule 'never claim improvement when deterministic metrics didn't improve' enforced directly: compareAttempts() reports only objective textual facts (word count delta, a number/outcome-language genuinely added, length change), never a fabricated quality score. Retry stores a new attempt_number, never overwrites the original. test:interview-retry-comparison 10/10 (deterministic, including a shorter/vaguer retry correctly reported as 'more concise' never 'improved') + test:interview-retry 8/8 (real browser + adversarial: cross-user 404, not-yet-completed-session 409).

requirements.description: Results page: Save-as-Story and Practice-Next additions are honest about the no-AI-score constraint
requirements.evidence: Save as Story copies the user's own real transcript answer verbatim into Story Bank — zero invention. Practice Next surfaces drill recommendations with an explicit caveat ('these aren't personalized to this session yet, since AI-powered scoring isn't enabled') rather than pretending to target real weaknesses that can't exist while Gemini analysis is disabled. test:interview-results-extras 5/5, verified independently via the real Story Bank page, not just a success toast.

requirements.description: WCAG AA color-contrast: Interview Lab pages + a pre-existing app-wide Sidebar bug
requirements.evidence: scripts/test-accessibility.mjs only ever scanned public marketing pages, so a contrast bug in the always-rendered Sidebar (text-muted-foreground/60 on the user's email and the billing upsell link, measured 2.85:1 and 3.51:1 by axe against the 4.5:1 AA threshold) had never been caught — it affects every authenticated page in the whole app, not just Interview Lab. Found via scripts/test-interview-accessibility.mjs (new), fixed in the Sidebar plus every text-muted-foreground/NN instance introduced in Interview Lab this session. test:interview-accessibility 10/10 (7 real authenticated pages + 3 manual keyboard/camera checks); test:accessibility (public pages) re-confirmed 10/10, no regression.

requirements.description: Age-eligibility gate removed at the user's explicit, confirmed instruction
requirements.evidence: The gate (DB columns, API checks, Lobby checkbox UI) existed to satisfy a stated Gemini ToS compliance requirement. Removed via migration 019 only after explicit user confirmation through AskUserQuestion (a destructive-migration auto-mode classifier had blocked the first attempt, citing the prior compliance requirement). This is a deliberate accepted-risk product decision by the account owner, not a verified compliance finding — documented here so the tradeoff is visible, not silently dropped.

requirements.description: Live voice mode (Gemini Live API) actually wired and tested end to end
requirements.blocker: Requires Gemini paid-project confirmation and legal/billing sign-off external to this codebase; isInterviewLiveEnabled() already fails closed without it (see IL-00).

requirements.description: Prompt-evaluation harness, scoring constitution document, golden dataset
requirements.blocker: Not yet built. Mostly meaningful only once Gemini analysis (isInterviewAnalysisEnabled) is re-enabled, since there are no real scored evaluations to validate a harness against today.

requirements.description: Visual QA screenshot review across Interview Lab's ~25 UI states
